sections.content: Modern VHF DSC radios can be connected to a GPS receiver to automatically include position and time in every DSC alert. This is especially critical for distress alerts, where an accurate position can save valuable time in the SAR response. If GPS is not connected, position must be entered manually.

rows: Degrees and decimal minutes, N or S
Degrees and decimal minutes, E or W
FOUR EIGHT DEGREES TWO SEVEN DECIMAL THREE MINUTES NORTH
Bearing 045° from Cape Finisterre, distance 15 NM
Use when exact lat/lon is unavailable

sections.content: Always verify that your GPS is feeding current position data to the DSC radio. A stale or missing GPS fix will be indicated on the radio display. In distress, an inaccurate position could direct rescuers to the wrong location.

sections.prompt: How is position typically expressed in maritime communications?

options.text: Degrees and minutes with N/S and E/W

sections.explanation: Maritime position is expressed in degrees, minutes, and decimal minutes with N/S for latitude and E/W for longitude (e.g., 48° 27.3' N, 004° 52.1' W).
